title: pelargonium × lingulatum
intro: Pelargonium × lingulatum, also known as South African tea or Cape rose, is a hybrid species within the Pelargonium genus, characterized by its distinctive heart-shaped leaves and fragrant flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in South Africa, due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Pelargonium × lingulatum include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and provide relief from digestive issues. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, and its essential oil can also be used in aromatherapy, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with other medications.
